title: Luscious Egyptian Ham Pie
description: A comforting, mustardy pie with flaky crust and a smoky hint from binchotan charcoal, perfect for a macrobiotic-friendly lunch.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200C and lightly oil a 9-inch pie dish.
Roll out the whole wheat pastry dough to fit the dish and line it, reserving a small piece for the top crust.
In a skillet, heat 1 tbsp olive oil over medium, sauté diced onion and minced garlic until fragrant, then add diced ham and cook for 3 minutes.
Stir in mustard, spinach, bay leaf, salt, pepper, and chickpea flour, then pour in vegetable broth and simmer until the mixture thickens, about 5 minutes.
Pour the ham mixture into the prepared crust, cover with remaining pastry, seal the edges, and cut slits for steam.
Bake for 30 minutes until golden and flaky, then remove from oven.
Sprinkle binchotan charcoal powder over the crust and return to oven for an additional 5 minutes to activate charcoal flavor and give a smoky finish.
